sentence_reviews.text: A compact predictive model of environment dynamics supports imagination, planning, and control.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Dreamer describes learning compact latent world models from experience and using imagined trajectories for actor-critic control across domains.
sentence_reviews.notes: The cited Dreamer source directly supports compact predictive dynamics, imagination, and control.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of Latent world models & predictive dynamics is to connect Model-based reinforcement learning, Latent variables & hidden state to downstream capabilities including Model-predictive control, World-model rollout & imagination, Game-playing, control & simulation agents.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Dreamer supports latent world models, imagined rollouts, and control; RL and PML sources support model-based RL and latent-variable background.
sentence_reviews.notes: The broad downstream list is graph-derived, but the main relationships are supported across the cited sources.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Model-based RL sources support predictive models for planning/control; Dreamer supports control from learned world models.
sentence_reviews.notes: Defensible, though MPC specifically is not the central Dreamer framing.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Dreamer trains behavior from imagined trajectories generated by the learned world model.
sentence_reviews.notes: Directly supported by the cited Dreamer source.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Game-playing, control & simulation agents
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Dreamer reports world-model control across diverse domains, including visual control benchmarks and game-like environments.
sentence_reviews.notes: Control and simulation-agent relevance is supported; game-playing breadth is a synthesis claim.

sentence_reviews.text: Its inductive bias, data demand, optimization behavior, and compute profile can make it unsuitable outside the regimes where it was validated. Its direct dependencies include Model-based reinforcement learning, Latent variables & hidden state.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Dreamer and RL/PML sources support model-based latent dynamics and general limitations around learned models and control.
sentence_reviews.notes: Generic limitation wording is defensible; exact unsuitability outside validation regimes is broader than any one cited claim.

sentence_reviews.text: Latent world models & predictive dynamics follows the learning setup expressed by Model-based reinforcement learning.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Dreamer is a model-based RL method using learned world models; Sutton & Barto covers model-based reinforcement learning.
sentence_reviews.notes: Directly supported by the cited sources.

sentence_reviews.text: Latent world models & predictive dynamics consumes, emits, or organizes information using Latent variables & hidden state.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Dreamer explicitly learns latent world models; PML supports latent-variable modeling background.
sentence_reviews.notes: Directly supported for latent world models.

sentence_reviews.text: Latent world models & predictive dynamics is assembled in part from the mechanism or model component State-space transitions.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Dreamer uses a recurrent state-space model for latent dynamics; PML supports state-space model background.
sentence_reviews.notes: Directly supported for Dreamer-style latent world models.

sentence_reviews.text: Latent world models & predictive dynamics follows the learning setup expressed by Generative modeling.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Dreamer learns a predictive generative world model in latent space; VAE/PML references support generative latent modeling background.
sentence_reviews.notes: The relationship is defensible, though 'trained_under' is taxonomy wording.

edge_reviews.notes: Dreamer directly supports world models as model-based RL.
Latent representation is core to the cited world-model source.
Dreamer-style recurrent state-space dynamics directly supports the edge.
World models are predictive generative models; common strength is defensible but taxonomy-level.
